an5.114:1.1: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Magadhans, at Andhakavinda.
an5.114:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat to one side. As Venerable Ānanda was sitting to one side, the *Bhagavā* said this to him:
an5.114:2.1: “Those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, who are new, recently gone forth, newly arrived in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] — those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, should be encouraged, settled, and established in five *dhammas*.
an5.114:2.3: ‘Come, friends, be virtuous, dwell restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, accomplished in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults; having undertaken them, train in the training rules’ — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in restraint by the Pātimokkha.
an5.114:3.1: ‘Come, friends, dwell with the doors guarded among the *indriyas*, with protective *sati*, with mature *sati*, with the mind [*mānasā*] well protected, endowed with a mind [*cetasā*] guarded by *sati*’ — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in restraint of the *indriyas* [*indriyasaṁvare*].
an5.114:4.1: ‘Come, friends, be of little speech, setting a limit to speech’ — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in setting a limit to speech.
an5.114:5.1: ‘Come, friends, be wilderness-dwellers; resort to remote lodgings in wilderness and forest groves’ — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in physical seclusion.
an5.114:6.1: ‘Come, friends, be ones of right view, endowed with right seeing’ — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in right seeing.
an5.114:6.2: Those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, who are new, recently gone forth, newly arrived in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] — those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, should be encouraged, settled, and established in these five *dhammas*.”
